name: BMM_TYPE
documentation: Abstract idea of specifying a type in some context. This is not the same as 'defining' a class. A type specification is essentially a reference of some kind, that defines the type of an attribute, or function result or argument. It may include generic parameters that might or might not be bound. See subtypes.

type_name.name: type_name
type_name.documentation: Formal string form of the type as per UML.

type_signature.name: type_signature
type_signature.documentation:
Signature form of the type name, which for generics includes generic parameter constrainer types E.g. `Interval<T:Ordered>`.

Defaults to the value of `_type_name()_`.

is_abstract.name: is_abstract
is_abstract.documentation: If true, indicates a type based on an abstract class, i.e. a type that cannot be directly instantiated.

is_primitive.name: is_primitive
is_primitive.documentation: If True, indicates that a type based solely on primitive classes.

unitary_type.name: unitary_type
unitary_type.documentation: Type with any container abstracted away; may be a formal generic type.

effective_type.name: effective_type
effective_type.documentation: Type with any container abstracted away, and any formal parameter replaced by its effective constraint type.

flattened_type_list.name: flattened_type_list
flattened_type_list.documentation: Completely flattened list of type names, flattening out all generic parameters.
